MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Apportionment Proceeding And For Order To Distribute Settlement Funds; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities; Declaration Of Mark Pifko, Esq.
Off Calendar Nov-21-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for hearing on the Motion for an Order to Apportion and Distribute Settlement Funds.
Dept. 514. Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Giselle Casey,CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

David B. Fernandes, Esq. appeared with plaintiffs Ruth Tubbs, Dawn Tubbs and Christopher Tubbs, Wrongful Death Heirs of Michael Tubbs, deceased.
Plaintiffs Ruth Tubbs, Dawn Tubbs and Christopher Tubbs who were appearing at today's hearing via CourtCall, were placed under oath and testified.

The Court, having reviewed the moving papers and having considered the evidence, both oral and documentary, and good cause appearing, RULES that the following are entitled to distribution of all net funds from settlements from the underlying survival/wrongful death claims, in the following proportions:

Ruth Tubbs - 75%.
Dawn Tubbs - 12.5%.
Christopher Tubbs - 12.5%.

Additionally, Baron & Budd shall recover from the net settlement funds recovered in this case the sum of two thousand five hundred sixty-eight ($2,568.00) dollars as and for reasonable attorney fees and costs incurred in this proceeding.

The balance of the settlement funds remaining after deduction of reasonable attorney fees and costs incurred in this proceeding shall be delivered by Baron & Budd to the heirs of Micheal Tubbs in the percentages set forth above.

All future net settlement funds from bankrupt defendants that have not yet settled, and those that have not yet paid agreed settlement sums, shall be distrubuted to the decendents' heirs in the percentages set forth above.

The signature of plaintiff Ruth Tubbs alone is sufficient to bind the estate and wrongful death heirs on all settlement documents.

Counsel for plaintiffs shall prepare the proposed Order to Apportion and Distribute Funds and submit same to the Court for reveiw and signature.
